We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accounting Office:
3Implement a Perpetual Inventory System. The municipality needs a system that tracks inventory quantities and values on an ongoing basis, not just at year-end. This includes recording additions (for regular purchases recorded as inventory) and deductions (issues/consumption – issuances are recorded as expense) as they occur. Recommendation on or before the target date of implementation.
4Implement Regular Inventory Reconciliation: Physical inventory counts should be conducted periodically (e.g., monthly or quarterly) and reconciled with the perpetual inventory records.
General Services Office:
5Address the corrupted software: Prioritize fixing or replacing the corrupted inventory software. The General Service Office will divert in employing new Inventory System, with the first initiative to visit the Province of Davao del Norte to conduct benchmarking on their existing Inventory System.
6Train Staff on Inventory Management: GSO personnel need comprehensive training on inventory management principles, including the use of a perpetual inventory system, proper handling of ledger cards, the software system, and the importance of accurate record-keeping.
7Re-evaluate Storage Space: The limited storage space issue needs to be addressed. If possible, explore options for expanding storage or reorganizing existing space to accommodate proper inventory storage and management.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
8Require the Petty Cash Custodians to replenish the petty cash fund when disbursements reach 75% of the authorized amount, or as needed as stated in Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. This will prevent operational disruptions due to insufficient petty cash;
9Re-evaluate petty cash fund policy and request for an increase of established Petty Cash Fund to cover unforeseen petty expenses, if necessary; and update the estimated need to ₱100,000.00.
10Immediately initiate the bonding process for the PCF Custodian in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal:
17ACoccnoduunctti nthgo Orofufgiche raencdo nGceilniaetriaoln S oefr vailcl eass sOeftfi,c ees ptheec ifaolll y o w thionsge: in the Property and Equipment for Distribution account; will produce the final listing of these PPEs to proceed on the transfer process.
18Establish and maintain accurate and up-to-date asset records; After the execution of necessary Sannguniang Bayan resolutions for those properties, the PPE Accounts will be updated accordingly.
19Establish a schedule for physical inventories and ensure adherence to it;
20Establish communication and coordination between the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office.
21Develop and implement formal procedures for documenting and processing asset transfer to other entities (Barangays, Schools) and system for the formal procedures for documenting and processing asset transfer to other entities.
22Ensure all asset transfer are properly documented with supporting documentation (e.g., transfer agreements and Sangguniang Bayan Resolutions) accordingly before the proper recording of those transfers.
23We also recommended that Management ensure any other properties not mentioned in this AOM but found under similar circumstances be derecognized in the Municipality’s books of accounts accordingly.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
24Prepare and submit a Waste Material Report (WMR) for all expired, damaged or obsolete inventory items to the Municipal Accounting Office for proper write-off and disposal;
25Ensure that all expired and damaged items are documented and disposed of properly to maintain inventory accuracy and expirations in the inventory records.
26Implement a regularized, documented physical inspection for all inventory items subject to expiration. A quarterly reconciliation of inventory which includes the updates on expirations and damages, will be conducted between the General Service Office and Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
27conduct comprehensive training for the Municipal Agriculture Office (MAGRO) staff on the proper preparation and maintenance of the Biological Assets Property Card (BAPC), in accordance with the Local Government Accounting Manual (LGAM) Volume I and Volume II;
28instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit promptly to the Accounting Office all necessary documents, such as Inventory Transfer Report or equivalent documents from donor agency for initial measurement of biological assets and regularly furnish the report on the changes in the fair value of the biological assets to ensure up-to-date fair value measurement of the biological assets for its subsequent recording; and
29direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant for timely recording of all receipts of donations in kind and its subsequent utilization or disposal in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to take immediate action to:
30provide a suitable warehouse or secure storage facility to properly store and protect medical supplies in line with good storage practices;
31implement regular monitoring and inventory inspections to ensure proper storage conditions are maintained, mitigating the risk of supply spoilage and waste; and
32ensure compliance with the DOH Supply Chain Management Service Warehouse Operations Manual to prevent financial loss and disrupt healthcare services within the community.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
33Implement a continuous reconciliation process, this involves consistently comparing physical inventory results with accounting records, investigating any discrepancies and conduct a reconciliation between the General Service Office and Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile the RPCPPE and the PPE account record of the Agency.
34Promptly adjusting both of books of accounts and property records accordingly.

We recommended and Management agreed to fast-track the implementation of a direct deposit payroll system as a means of improving payroll processing and reducing associated risks. SB Resolution and Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and the Depository Bank will be executed to establish the direct deposit payroll system among all employees, Regular and Job Order. Agreement between the LGU and the Depository Bank will be executed to establish the direct deposit payroll system among all employees, Regular and Job Order.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to stop charging such administrative expenses to the 5% LDRRMF in pursuant to the provisions Section 4.3 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular instead it shall be sourced from the General fund of the Municipality. An Office Order/Memorandum Order will be issued detailing a regular personnel to be assigned particularly in controilling all transactions under the 5% LDRRMF in pursuant to the provisions Section 4.3 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular to ensure the proper charging of those transactions in the LDRRMF.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct:
1. The Office of the Municipal Accountant to:
37a. Process refunds immediately upon verifying the correct computation.
38b. For Due to Pag-IBIG Accounts: If identified as an over deduction, initiate the refund process without delay.
39c. For Due to PhilHealth Accounts: Prepare the necessary journal entries and reconcile balances to ensure accuracy.
40d. Strengthen internal controls by conducting regular reconciliation of withheld and remitted amounts to prevent recurring under-remittances. An Office Order will be issued by the Municipal Accountant to Assign personnel as in-charge in the Reconciliation of these accounts.
2. The Human Resource Management Office to:
41a. Coordinate with GSIS for the immediate updating of salary records and ensure the submission of updated payroll records, salary adjustments, and the latest Agency Remittance Advice (ARA).
42b. Request an updated GSIS billing/Statement of Account (SOA) reflecting the correct salary basis.
43c. Conduct regular reconciliation with GSIS to prevent discrepancies in remittances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
44Require the concerned personnel to submit Daily Time Records (DTRs) and Accomplishment Reports (ARs) with complete and authenticated entries, or an official certification from their immediate supervisor or facility head, along with any supporting documents, to justify the previously disbursed HEA; and

compliance of those documentations. compliance of those documentations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the :
46A. Municipality conduct a thorough inventory of all land assets, ensuring that each parcel is documented with precise boundaries, unique identifiers (parcel numbers, tax IDs), physical addresses, land area measurements, and legal descriptions. All existing documentation, including deeds, titles, and survey records, should be compiled and verified for accuracy;
47B. immediately perform a comprehensive reconciliation of land asset valuations between the MAO and MPDO records. Any discrepancies resulting from differing valuation methods to align with the requirements of Section 209 of Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Unit – Volume 1 – Criteria for Recognition of Property, Plant and Equipment; and
48C. immediately prioritize and allocate sufficient funding within the municipal budget to address all outstanding land titling issues. This includes funding for legal fees, documentation costs, and any necessary procedural expenses.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Human Resource Management Office to immediately prioritize the development and implementation of a comprehensive organizational structure and staffing plan. This plan clearly defines roles, responsibilities and reporting lines for all positions and align staffing with municipality’s strategic goals and service delivery objectives. By strengthening the HRMO and implementing these recommendations, the municipality can ensure a more efficient, effective, and compliant workforce.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit a letter justifying the non-preparation of the GAD Agenda, in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circulars 2013-01 and 2016-01. The justification should include reasons for the omission, encountered challenges, and steps to ensure future compliance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive creates an Inventory Committee to conduct the inventory of road networks in compliance with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and an Inventory Committee through an Executive Order to conduct the road network inventory. Submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for evaluation and verification. After the process of LRAM process in compliance to COA Circular No. 2015-008 the corresponding reports along with the Adjusting JEV from the General Service Office/ Municipal Assessor's Office/ Municipal Engineering Office—were to be submitted to the audit team for review.

The local executives shall include in the annual budget the appropriation for the programs/ projects/ activities such as, but not limited to, the provision of a sanitary landfill in compliance to the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is waiting for the program of Provincial Government Plan to have a provincial wide sanitary landfill because the LGU’s location is not feasible to maintain a sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The LGU still failed to provide for a final disposal site in compliance to the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issues an Order authorizing a certain officer to secure bank statements from the depository bank on a regular basis.  Meantime, the Accounting Office should exert due efforts to prepare bank reconciliation statements based on snapshots of the accounts in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended management to adhere to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the time frame in which the accountable officers should liquidate their cash advances.  If circumstances warrant, the Local Chief Executive should cause the withholding of salaries of the defaulting accountable officers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004.

We recommended that management make representation with DLPC management to submit Memorandum of Agreement showing the rate of refunds.  Also, submit Demand Letter requiring the DLPC to refund the advances amounting to P5,065,590.48.  Otherwise, file appropriate charges against the responsible officials pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that the Accounting Office reclassify the fund transfers from “Other Payables” (2-99-99-990)” account to “Due to LGUs” 2-02-01-070” account.  Also, report the funds received in accordance with the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 94-013 which the preparation of Memorandum of Agreement duly signed by both parties.
It was further recommended that the residual amount of P141,021.33 should be returned to the Source Agency if the purpose of the fund transfers had been realized.

We recommended that management call the attention of the contractors to submit the billing documents relative to the completed projects.  Upon submission, the Accounting Office should adjust the account “Due to Other NGAs” effecting the completion of projects and liquidation of the fund transfers in the amount of P14,946,388.43 and recognize the depreciation expenses in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office coordinate with the MTO and cause the reconciliation of the Due from/Due to other Funds accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto, to correct the balances of the intra-agency accounts.

We recommended that Officials responsible in the preparation of annual appropriation to refrain from allocating budget for repairs and maintenance of roads chargeable to the 20% development fund in compliance with DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.  Such program of expenditures should be properly charged against the general fund of the Municipality.
We recommended for the immediate liquidation of the fund transfer in the amount of P100,000.00 and prepare accounting entries to close “Other Payables” account in the books of accounts.

We recommended that responsible officials of the Municipality observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 on the appropriation and utilization of the 5% Calamity Fund to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.

We recommended that coordination with the Officials of Braulio E. Dujali Fire Station Officials shall be made to demand the release of 20% share in revenues arising from the enforcement of RA No. 9514.  Further, require the Braulio E. Dujali Fire Station to submit copies of its Monthly Report of Collections and Deposits to the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate the monitoring and accounting of the LGU’s 20% share from the Fire Code Revenues.

It is recommended that representation shall be made with the management of GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth for reconciliation of correct balances as of December 31, 2018.  From these balances, the Accounting Office has to prepare reconciliation of the LGU’s record.
Henceforth, remit the mandatory contributions to GSIS, Pagibig and Philhealth within the set deadline to avoid further incurrence of variances.

We recommended that Accounting Office keeps its records based on documents attached to duly approved disbursement vouchers or its equivalent to avoid further incurrence of variances.  Henceforth, avoid partial remittance of the taxes withheld in compliance with Section 251 of Tax Reform Act of 1997.

We recommended that the Municipality submit to the office of the auditor the annual GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 and the years thereafter within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

We recommended for the immediate implementation of the projects, programs and activities funded out by Fund Transfers.  Also, the unspent balance of the funds shall be returned to the Source Agencies as required by laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to submit the updated status of work accomplished of all PPAs to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason:
The LGU did not fully implement the PPAs funded out by fund transfers.

DSWD – Flood Control P3,774,313

 DILG - Flood Control P3,986,175

DILG - Potable Water P9,600,000

observations.recommendation: Budget allocation for Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund shall exclude revenues derived from the operation of Local Economic Enterprise.  Otherwise, the misappropriated amount shall be disallowed in audit.
We recommended that management observe the timeliness in the remittance of the provincial share in the SEF collection within 15 days of every succeeding month.

Accountable officers be required to liquidate their cash advances totaling P203,560.19 to forestall issuance of audit suspension.  Henceforth, no cash advance should be granted to accountable officers with existing cash advances in compliance to COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant committed to coordinate with the Honorable Mayor for possible issuance of sanction or legal remedy.
observations.reason: Advances to Officers and Employees were not fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: Management make representation with the suppliers on the existence and validity of Account Payables totaling P801,007.75. Otherwise, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer should stop setting up invalid obligations.
observations.managementAction: The Management will discontinue the practice of setting up obligations at the time the documentary requirements were already processed.
observations.reason: The Accounts Payable balance was not fully settled.

observations.recommendation: The Accounting Office shall establish and maintain special account for the Local Economic Enterprises.  Anent thereto, financial data shall be submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan as a basis for proper appropriation of the annual budget of the Municipality.

The Municipal Accountant should maintain Special Account in the General Fund including Subsidiary Ledgers exclusively for the 20% Development Fund for proper monitoring of projects implemented under this fund.

Management should consider the possibility of establishing a LDRRM Office with assigned Officer and staff pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.  Likewise, the LDRRM Council should revisit the prior year’s MDRRM Plan and implement programs/ projects not yet fully implemented.

Recommendations:

a.	Require the former Local Chief Executive and BAC members to submit justifications why payments were effected despite lack of legal basis and incomplete documentations.  Also, submit Contract Agreement showing the rate of refunds to be made by DLPC, otherwise, require the refund of the whole amount of P7,482,010.85.

b.	Ensure that all the processes and procedures in the procurement of goods and other deliverables are in compliance with RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR to avoid irregularities.

c.	Submit records showing the advance payments to, and refunds made by, the DLPC, otherwise, file appropriate charges against the responsible officials pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: The Management had made agreement with DLPC regarding the refunds of advance payments on installment basis.
observations.reason: Advance payments were not fully refunded to the Municipality having a balance of P5,065,590.48 as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: Management should create a Committee tasked to dispose of thru public auction 39 unserviceable properties totaling P618,567.90 and those 79 unserviceable properties without historical cost pursuant to the provision of Section 79 of PD 1445.  Further, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV reclassifying the unserviceable assets to the “Other Assets” account.
observations.managementAction: Constituted Inventory and Appraisal Committee                                  to conduct training on physical counting of properties.
observations.reason: Properties under question were not disposed of yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should consider the following corrective measures:

Establish a LDRRM Office with assigned officer and staff pursuant to NDRRMC/ DILG/ DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

Strictly adhere to the guidelines required under NDRRMC/DBM/DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the utilization of Trust Fund under LDRRMF.
